chain.thought:
Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
the youngest living president -> president, age at start date

The question is asking to determine who was the youngest living president at the start of their presidency. To find out, we need to sort the data based on the "age at start date" column in the order of "small to large" using the f_sort_column(age at start date) operation. This will allow us to see the presidents ordered from youngest to oldest based on their age at the start of their presidency.
Theodore Roosevelt was 49 years, 241 days old at the start of his presidency, which is the youngest age among all presidents listed. Therefore, Theodore Roosevelt was the youngest living president.

judge_thought:
Explanation: 
To determine the youngest living president, we need to look at the age at which each president became the oldest living president (which indicates they were the youngest among the living presidents at that time).
From the original table:
- Theodore Roosevelt became the oldest living president on June 24, 1908, at the age of 49 years, 241 days.
Comparing this to others:
George Washington was 57 years, 67 days.
John Adams was 64 years, 45 days.
James Madison was 75 years, 110 days.
Andrew Jackson was 69 years, 105 days.
Theodore Roosevelt has the youngest recorded age at which he became the oldest living president, indicating he was the youngest living president at that time.
